problem_statement: A community food bank's refrigerated loading dock remained marked occupied after a coordinator failover, preventing five outbound deliveries from receiving a dock assignment even though the bay was physically empty.

observations.statement: Dock D4 was reserved for delivery V217 at 14:07:12; the active coordinator lost power at 14:07:14, its standby took over at 14:07:19, and the first false occupied response occurred at 14:08:03.
observations.provenance: Merged coordinator journal, UPS log, and dispatch request ledger with sequence numbers preserved.

observations.statement: The pre-repair reservation export shows D4 assigned to owner session s-8841 with no expiry, while the post-failover session registry contains no s-8841 entry and no delivery claims that session.
observations.provenance: Read-only exports taken by separate storage and dispatch operators before remediation.

observations.statement: The journal records reservation creation before power loss and a handheld release acknowledgement after takeover, but the acknowledgement references the old coordinator epoch and did not alter the D4 record.
observations.provenance: Checksummed append-only journal copied from both coordinator volumes.

observations.statement: Door contact, occupancy beam, and camera review all show V217 leaving D4 by 14:07:42 and the bay remaining empty until operators manually held subsequent vehicles.
observations.provenance: Three independently maintained physical-safety records reviewed by dock operations.

observations.statement: Restoring the captured reservation export into an isolated coordinator reproduced the occupied response for D4; a clean store under the same build and settings accepted the identical assignment request.
observations.provenance: Paired offline replay with gate actuators and dispatch notifications disabled.

observations.statement: Applying a preconditioned repair to only the ownerless D4 record in the isolated copy allowed all five blocked assignments while leaving the other 13 active dock reservations byte-identical.
observations.provenance: Dry-run repair reviewed by a second operator before any live action.

observations.statement: Scanner connectivity, coordinator CPU and memory, database replication lag, and dock sensor health remained normal before and after the failover.
observations.provenance: Infrastructure and facilities monitors maintained outside the dispatch service.

observations.statement: All 13 legitimate reservations had live owner sessions and bounded expiries; D4 was the only record combining an absent owner with no expiry.
observations.provenance: Invariant report generated from the preserved pre-repair export.

observations.statement: After the reviewed repair was applied live, the five deliveries received valid assignments, and two supervised coordinator failovers completed with no ownerless reservations in the following 48 hours.
observations.provenance: Signed remediation record and post-change invariant monitor.
